title: Pre-work
text:
Before the workshop, please complete the following steps to prepare (more details in the sections below):
If you have trouble with any of the above steps or have other questions, please reach out in the #workshop-quarto-2026 channel, after joining the main conference Slack.

title: Pre-work
section: 1. Get the latest pre-release version of Quarto
text:
1. Get the latest pre-release version of Quarto
Install the latest pre-release, v1.10.*, from: https://quarto.org/docs/download/prerelease.html

title: Pre-work
section: 2. Decide your publishing destination and get an account if needed
text:
2. Decide your publishing destination and get an account if needed
I’ll be demonstrating publishing your website. To follow along you’ll need an account on a hosting platform. Pick from these two options:

GitHub Pages: Choose this if you are already a GitHub user, and are comfortable creating a repo, and pushing to it. No additional setup required.
Posit Connect Cloud: Otherwise, choose this. Sign up for a free account at https://connect.posit.cloud.

title: Pre-work
text:
3. Collect some content
There are a few things you might like to collect before the workshop:

A paragraph about who you are (or what your site is about)
A photo/image you would like on your home page
A list of projects, talks, classes, or publications
Any existing Quarto documents you would like to include in your site

title: Pre-work
section: 4. Get up to speed with Quarto
text:
4. Get up to speed with Quarto
Before taking the workshop, you should be able to:

Preview or render Quarto documents
Identify the YAML header, Markdown content, and code cells in a Quarto document
Specify Quarto options with YAML syntax
Specify files using relative paths

If you need to catch up, complete one of the following:

Complete the Quarto chapter in R for Data Science
Complete the Getting Started Tutorial on the Quarto documentation site
Watch the first 16mins of Getting Started with Quarto on YouTube

title: Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Friday, June 26, 2026, 1:30–4:30 PM
Miller Center, 615 SW Harrison Street, Portland, OR
Jump to Pre-work Jump to slides

title: Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Description
As a data professional, you’ll eventually want a website—whether for yourself, a project, or a research group. Quarto is an excellent way to build one: create your site with plain text files that you can edit in your favorite tool and check into version control; organize your content into folders and pages in a way that makes sense to you; create content that includes code and its output; and generate listing pages that help readers navigate collections of content like projects, publications, or people.
In this workshop, you’ll walk through the process from scratch. You’ll see how to get started with a basic template and then customize it to your needs, including adding pages, customizing navigation, adding a blog or other listing, personalizing the appearance, and getting it online.
This workshop will be hands-on, and you’ll need to prepare a few things before it begins. Look for instructions closer to the workshop date. It will help if you’ve already used Quarto to produce documents, but I’ll provide resources to get you up to speed beforehand if you haven’t. You don’t need any HTML, CSS/SCSS, or Git/GitHub experience, nor do you need any particular programming language (R, Python, etc.) or level of programming experience.

title: Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your Instructor
Dr. Charlotte Wickham (she/her) is a Developer Advocate on the Quarto team at Posit, PBC. As part of her job at Posit, Charlotte helps to keep quarto.org—a website about, and also built with, Quarto—up to date. Prior to Posit, she taught Statistics and Data Science at Oregon State University, where she received awards for her in-person and online teaching.

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
section: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
From Zero to Website with Quarto



Find the Slack channel #workshop-quarto-2026 in the main conference Slack
Check you’ve completed the steps on the Pre-work page.


Workshop website 

    


Workshop website: https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Hello, I’m Charlotte Wickham


Developer Advocate
 @ 
Based in Corvallis, OR 
 cvwickham
 cwickham
 charlotte.wickham@posit.co

Workshop website 

    


https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your Turn

Introduce yourself to your neighbors:

Name
Location
What you need a website for

Check you are all set up


 

https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Outline
Goal: You create a website with Quarto

Don’t start from scratch
Add pages and customize navigation
Make the style your own
Publish
Use listings for “lists” of content

BREAK: 3:00 – 3:15pm

https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
What to expect
My turns: I’ll use slides, and live code to demonstrate ideas.
Your Turns: You practice what you’ve just seen.
Ask questions:

During Your Turns
After Your Turns
In Slack channel


https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Demo: Start from a template
From a new empty project:


Terminal

quarto use template cwickham/website-starter

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
section: Your Turn: Get the starter template
text:
Your Turn: Get the starter template


Start a new project.
RStudio > New Project
Positron > New Folder from Template > Empty Project
Get the template:


Terminal

quarto use template cwickham/website-starter

Create a subdirectory for template? No
Open and preview index.qmd.
Match the content in index.qmd to the preview.

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Demo: Exploring index.qmd

Render/preview index.qmd. It becomes index.html, your homepage.
Regular Quarto document with:

YAML Header
Content, possibly including code cells

Workflow: Edit, render/preview
Uses an about template: https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-about.html
Other templates: jolla, trestles, solana, marquee, or broadside

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your Turn

Edit index.qmd:

Change the title and subtitle
Try a different template: jolla, trestles, solana, marquee, or broadside
Edit, add, or remove items in links. Find other icons at https://icons.getbootstrap.com/
Challenge: Use a different image, and edit image-alt

Spare time? Edit the content of the page

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Demo: Use a different image

Move new image into project
Replace path
Edit image-alt

Paths in a .qmd are relative to the location of the .qmd

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
What is a Quarto website?
A minimal website only needs two files:




_quarto.yml

project: 
  type: website

Tells Quarto this is a website project



index.qmd

---
title: My homepage
---

Becomes index.html the homepage

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
section: What else is in this template?
text:
What else is in this template?
website/
├── _quarto.yml   
├── index.qmd      
├── profile.jpg   # Used in `index.qmd` 
├── _brand.yml    # Theming
├── styles.scss   # Custom styles, none defined`
├── _examples/    # For teaching, not in website
├── README.md     # For GitHub, not in website
└── _site/        # Fully rendered site, .gitignored

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Demo: Add a page

New Quarto Document: projects.qmd


projects.qmd

---
title: Projects
---

Some projects I've worked on:

File path becomes URL path: { site url }/projects.html

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Demo: Add navigation
_quarto.yml: configuration file


_quarto.yml

website:
  navbar:
    right: 
      - projects.qmd

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Folder structure determines URLs
website/  
├── _quarto.yml
├── index.qmd             { base url }
├── about.qmd             { base url }/about.html  
├── reports/                       
|    ├── index.qmd        { base url }/reports/   
|    └── last-month.qmd   { base url }/reports/last-month.html 
└── data/                 
    └── sales.csv         { base url }/data/sales.csv
{ base url }: http://localhost:XXXX when previewing locally

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your Turn


Add a page
Add a link to the page in the navigation
Skim https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-navigation.html. What other types of navigation are available?

Spare time: Add content to the page

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Demo: Navigation

Put items on left
Switch to a sidebar with content and back again
Edit tools

Quick reference: https://quarto-tdg.org/websites.html#other-navigation-elements

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Use brand.yml: Color


_brand.yml

color:
  foreground: "#333333"  # font color
  background: "#ffffff"  # background color
  primary: "#333333"     # navbar and links

Demo: Change primary
Positron command: Show or Focus Standalone Color Picker
Brand Guide: https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/brand.html

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Use brand.yml: Fonts


_brand.yml

typography:
  fonts:
    - family: Open Sans
      source: bunny
    - family: Roboto Slab
      source: bunny
  base:
    family: Open Sans
  headings:
    family: Roboto Slab

Demo: Add these to _brand.yml
bunny: Bunny Fonts, zero-tracking and no-logging policy https://fonts.bunny.net

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Demo: Check accessibility with axe
Once you start playing with colors it’s easy to create contrast issues.
Check with axe-core by adding:


_quarto.yml

format:
  html:
    axe:
      output: document

Remove before publishing!

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your Turn


Edit _brand.yml to customize colors and fonts
Enable the axe checks to check for contrast issues


Brand Guide: https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/brand.html#brand-elements

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Use brand.yml: Code
If your pages have code, you will probably want to tweak monospace options:


_brand.yml

typography:
  monospace-inline:
    color: "#6b21a8"
    background-color: background 
  monospace-block:
    background-color: "#eaf2fb"

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Use brand.yml: Dark mode
Add light and dark to colors to add a dark mode toggle to your site:


_brand.yml

color:
  foreground: 
    light: "#333333"
    dark: "#ffffff"
  background: 
    light: "#ffffff"
    dark: "#333333"
  primary: 
    light: "#333333"
    dark: "#ffffff"

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
section: Name colors under color: palette to more easily refer to them
text:
Name colors under color: palette to more easily refer to them


_brand.yml

color:
  palette:
    white: "#ffffff"
    grey: "#333333"
    pink: "#b70ca6"
  foreground: grey
  background: white
  primary: grey

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
section: Sometimes you might need to add SCSS

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Aside: The three parts of _quarto.yml


_quarto.yml

project: # Project options 
  type: website

website: # Website options 
  title: "My Website"
  search: false
  open-graph: true # https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-tools.html#open-graph
  page-footer: "Made with ❤️ and Quarto"
  navbar:
    right:
      - text: Home
        href: index.qmd
    tools: 
      - icon: github
        text: Github
        href: https://github.com

format: # Page 'html' format options
  html:
    theme: [brand, styles.scss]    

Reference: Projects > Websites, and Formats > HTML

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Demo: Use quarto publish


Terminal

quarto publish posit-connect-cloud

Other venues: gh-pages, netlify, connect
Updates? Re-run quarto publish
Pick one of:

Your Turn: Posit Connect Cloud
Your Turn: GitHub Pages


https://quarto.org/docs/publishing/

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your Turn: Posit Connect Cloud


Publish your site to Posit Connect Cloud:


Terminal

quarto publish posit-connect-cloud

Challenge: find the setting to customize the URL.

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your Turn: GitHub Pages


Make your project a Git repo.
Commit, and push to GitHub
Publish to GitHub Pages:


Terminal

quarto publish gh-pages

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
section: Which venue is right for you?
text:
Which venue is right for you?









Provider
Private Source
Private Site
Custom Domain




Posit Connect Cloud
✔️
Paid plans
Paid plans


GitHub
Paid plans
GitHub Enterprise Cloud only
✔️


Netlify
✔️
Paid plans
✔️



Or Posit Connect if your org has it.

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Other publishing workflows

Posit Connect Cloud:

Use Posit Publisher extension in Positron or VS Code
Connect directly to GitHub repo

Continuous integration: https://quarto-tdg.org/websites.html#sec-ci

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Website metadata: search engines
Set title, description and site-url.
E.g. for this workshop’s website:


_quarto.yml

website:
  title: Zero to Website with Quarto
  description: "Zero to website with Quarto Workshop for Cascadia R Conf 2026"
  site-url: https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Website metadata: social cards
Add a 1200 × 630 px image and image-alt:




_quarto.yml

website:
  image: thumbnail.jpeg
  image-alt: "Zero to Website with Quarto with a screenshot of a website titled 'Alicia, Data Scientist'."
  open-graph: true

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
A listing is…

an automatically generated list of content
styled via a template, (built-in type, or custom template)
can be included on any page


https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-listings.html

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Use listings for …

BlogPeoplePublications












See more examples at: https://charlotte.quarto.pub/listings/

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
section: Listing contents: can be a YAML file
text:
Listing contents: can be a YAML file
Items link to path.


_examples/listing-talks/talks.yml

- date: 2025-03-12
  title: "From Notebook to Production: Deploying ML Models with Confidence"
  description: "A practical walkthrough of taking a model out of a Jupyter notebook and into a reproducible, monitored production pipeline—covering containers, CI/CD, and drift detection."
  venue: "PyData Seattle"
  path: "https://example.com/talks/notebook-to-production"

- date: 2024-11-08
  title: "The Art of the Reproducible Report"
  description: "Why your analysis should run end-to-end with a single command, and how literate programming tools like Quarto make stakeholder-ready reports painless."
  venue: "posit::conf"
  path: "https://example.com/talks/reproducible-report"

- date: 2024-06-04
  title: "Causal Inference for the Skeptical Analyst"
  description: "Correlation isn't causation, but we can do better than throwing up our hands. An accessible tour of difference-in-differences, propensity scores, and when to trust them."
  venue: "useR! Conference"
  path: "https://example.com/talks/causal-inference-skeptical-analyst"

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Listing contents: can other pages
Items link to page.


_examples/listing-projects
└──projects
    ├── coastal-sensors.qmd
    ├── forest-canopy.qmd
    ├── watershed-forecasting.qmd
    └── images



projects/coastal-sensors.qmd

---
title: "Coastal Sensor Network"
description: "Real-time monitoring of water quality along the Oregon coast using a fleet of low-cost IoT sensors."
image: images/photo-1601698832159-665cbefd11da.jpg
categories: [IoT, Time Series, Dashboards]
---

## Overview

Placeholder content. This project deploys a network of sensors that stream
temperature, salinity, and turbidity readings into a central pipeline, surfaced
through a live dashboard for researchers and the public.

Photo by [Karsten Winegeart](https://unsplash.com/@_karsten) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/photos/_m-Xjo9R_y4).

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your turn: Brainstorm a listing


Brainstorm a list of content you might want to include in your website.
What are the key pieces of information for each item? E.g. title, description, … etc.
Does it make sense to give items each their own page, or to use a YAML file?


See more examples at: https://charlotte.quarto.pub/listings/

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Adding a listing page
A listing page is a regular page that uses the listing option.




projects.qmd

---
title: Projects
listing:
  contents: projects
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 2
  categories: true
  filter-ui: false
  sort-ui: false
---




talks.qmd

---
title: Talks
listing:
  contents: talks.yml
  type: table
  filter-ui: false
  sort-ui: false
---

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your turn: Add a listing


Drag one of _examples/listing-projects or _examples/listing-talks into your website.
Edit at least one item to match your listing idea.
Edit the listing page talks.qmd/projects.qmd to reflect your list.
Play with some of the listing options.


Listing documentation: Types and Options

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Your turn: Keep building!


Keep tweaking the content of pages
Delete pages you don’t want (and delete any related nav from _quarto.yml)
Double-check _quarto.yml
Publish any updates

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Quarto Websites
A minimal website only needs two files:




_quarto.yml

project: 
  type: website




index.qmd

---
title: My homepage
---

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:
Building a website

Add pages by adding Quarto documents
Control navigation, website settings, etc. in _quarto.yml
Control colors and fonts in _brand.yml
Two special kinds of pages: listings and about pages

title: From Zero to Website with Quarto
text:

Learn from other people’s website source: https://quarto.org/docs/gallery/#websites
